records.ety: [F. dcamper; pref. d- (L. dis) + camp camp. See Camp.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Decamped (?; 215); p. pr. & vb. n. Decamping.]

defs: To break up a camp; to move away from a camping ground, usually by night or secretly.
Hence, to depart suddenly; to run away; -- generally used disparagingly.
quotes: The fathers were ordered to decamp, and the house was once again converted into a tavern.
